mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 6.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, driving earnings revisions upward and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 6.0 within a 3-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in industrial production and residential housing starts signals synchronized supply-demand recovery, which compresses risk premiums and lifts equity valuations over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a random baseline in out-of-sample testing, with directional accuracy dropping below 52% over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a rising rate environment, a contraction in real consumer credit growth signals tightening financial conditions that restrict corporate investment and consumer demand, depressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive or neutral when both FEDFUNDS and RSAFS move in the specified direction, yielding a negative sign IC over any 12-month period.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP expands the corporate earnings base, which mechanically supports equity valuations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The sign of GDPC1 growth consistently predicts negative 60-day forward returns in more than 60% of observations across multiple decades.

mechanism_narrative: When nonfarm payrolls exceed 200,000 for two consecutive months, robust employment expansion validates economic resilience, reducing equity risk premiums and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly change > 200000 for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero or negative when the payroll threshold is breached, with a t-statistic below 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial output coinciding with stable consumer prices indicates benign supply-side expansion without inflationary pressure, which allows the Federal Reserve to maintain accommodative policy and supports equity valuations over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative forward returns in over 55% of cases, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression yields a statistically insignificant coefficient (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: During periods of accelerating real GDP growth, a simultaneous deceleration in housing starts signals capital reallocation away from residential construction toward commercial and industrial investment, which boosts corporate profitability and lifts S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative when GDP growth accelerates while housing construction declines, indicating that the capital reallocation mechanism does not materialize in equity pricing.
